meta.title: Noise Exposure Assessment & Hearing-Conservation Plan
meta.subtitle: Exposure-zone map, action-level comparison & audiometric-surveillance schedule
meta.author: HSE Manufacturing — Occupational Health
meta.classification: Internal — special-category audiometric health data — competent-person (occupational hygienist / OH physician) review required
meta.deid_notice: All workers are referred to by similar-exposure-group (SEG) or role label. Audiometric / standard-threshold-shift results are reported by SEG with <5 small-cell suppression; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: This assessment covers the named area and similar-exposure groups (SEGs) below, mapped into noise-exposure zones against the cited action and limit values. Exposure values are transcribed measured/estimated 8-hr TWA L_EX,8h dB(A); no dosimetry is computed by this skill. Audiometric data is reported by SEG, never by individual.

sections.caption: Exposure-zone map — transcribed values vs cited thresholds
Each exposure compared to a cited threshold (authority+year); transcribed, not computed

rows.control: Substitute / retrofit a quieter die-cushion and damp the press frame (source/engineering)
Acoustic enclosure / barrier around the line-2 press (engineering)
Job rotation + time-limited exposure for the operator SEG (administrative)
Issue and fit-test hearing protection (HPD) — LAST resort, not the sole control

sections.text: Hearing protection is the LAST resort: for the ≥ 85 dBA SEGs the source/engineering controls above must be assessed first — HPD alone does not satisfy the hierarchy.

items: SEGs ≥ 85 dBA enrolled in the hearing-conservation program
Mandatory hearing-protection zones signed for ≥ 90 dBA areas
Re-survey on any process / equipment change

rows: Retest within 30 days; if STS confirmed → review controls + refer
sections.caption: Action-level-linked audiometry — reported by SEG, <5 cells suppressed

rows.action: Commission acoustic enclosure on the line-2 press
Enrol the ≥ 85 dBA SEGs and run baseline audiometry
Re-survey noise after the enclosure is installed

items: [GAP] Maintenance-SEG exposure is estimated from equipment data — confirm by ISO 9612 measurement.
[ASSUMPTION] 8-hr TWA basis; confirm impulsive/peak (dB C) for the press if applicable.

sections.text: Decision-support only. A competent person (occupational hygienist / audiometric technician / OH physician) must review and sign off this assessment. Review cadence: annual or on any exposure change / confirmed STS.

items: US OSHA 29 CFR 1910.95 — occupational noise exposure
